entities.name: Ghost Jobs
entities.context: Job postings with no intent to hire, estimated at 18-22% of all postings

entities.name: Tiered Response Strategy
entities.context: Framework for allocating effort: high effort for high-alignment roles, streamlined approach for others

entities.name: Speed Plus Personalization
entities.context: Belief that speed combined with personalization beats pure volume in job applications

entities.name: Pipeline Building
entities.context: One reason companies post ghost jobs — to accumulate candidate pipelines without active hiring need

entities.name: Investor Signaling
entities.context: Companies post ghost jobs to signal growth or hiring momentum to investors

entities.name: Internal Compliance
entities.context: Companies post ghost jobs to satisfy internal policies requiring open postings

entities.name: Personal Brand Dilution
entities.context: Risk that mass-applying to jobs weakens a candidate's perceived quality and positioning

entities.name: Strategic Targeting
entities.context: Approach of identifying and pursuing real opportunities rather than applying broadly

entities.name: Hiring Market
entities.context: The domain this position addresses — systemic dynamics of job postings and candidate behavior

entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Holder of this position; career coach analyzing hiring market dysfunction

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company, context for his expertise on hiring market dynamics

relationships.context: Identifies ghost jobs as a systemic problem affecting 18-22% of postings
Advocates tiered effort allocation as the rational response to ghost jobs
Holds that speed plus personalization beats volume in job seeking
Warns that mass-applying dilutes personal brand
Advocates strategic targeting of real opportunities over volume

Investor signaling is a corporate motive behind ghost job postings
Internal compliance requirements drive some ghost job postings

Tiered response is the prescribed countermeasure to the ghost jobs problem
